text: Under MARPOL Annex I regulations, a "Combination Carrier" type vessel is defined as a ship designed to carry:

options.text: General break-bulk cargo and passengers together
Either oil or solid bulk cargoes in its cargo spaces
Containers and heavy machinery on deck simultaneously
Liquefied gases and chemical compounds in independent tanks

explanation: A combination carrier (such as an OBO - Ore-Bulk-Oil carrier) is a vessel specifically designed to be flexible, allowing it to carry either liquid cargoes like oil or solid bulk cargoes like ore in the same cargo spaces, depending on operational needs.
